subject: [UUI] New Solve 2 components in one Docker
commit_message:
[UUI] New Solve 2 components in one Docker

1.add postgres env in uui-server
2.add server init sql in uui-postgres

Signed-off-by: dujunyi <hunterdevil@foxmail.com>
Issue-ID: USECASEUI-405
Change-Id: I28654f2cd87c88d0ce915f74c7736e58583d6925

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

It is common practice to make changes to the original commit and reply to any code review comments.
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/130214

Without them we lose historical changes to the patch.

Also, could you please build, deploy and test this change locally before pushing to gerrit.
On each patch set, a new set of gating runs are triggered which uses unnecessary resources.

Patch Set 3:

well,i followed your tips to use oom postgres, but i met the following errors:

i use 
`Make SKIP_LINT=TRUE uui`
to build helm charts
then i use
`helm install dev-uui local/uui --set globalPassword=123456 -n uui`
the readiness probe cannot get access to init-postgres. it logs  http 403.
and my server didn`t start.
i found it met same situation in vnfsdk ,when i use command:
`helm install dev-vnfsdk local/vnfsdk --set globalPassword=123456 -n uui`
the readiness probe cannot get access to init-postgres. it logs  http 403.


i thought it might be questions about my local env. so i committed once for test. 
therefore,i found no logs about my first three commit-lint check. Is there any tips about recheck my lint error?

> well,i followed your tips to use oom postgres, but i met the following errors:
> 
> i use 
> `Make SKIP_LINT=TRUE uui`
> to build helm charts
> then i use

> the readiness probe cannot get access to init-postgres. it logs  http 403.
> and my server didn`t start.

> `helm install dev-vnfsdk local/vnfsdk --set globalPassword=123456 -n uui`
> the readiness probe cannot get access to init-postgres. it logs  http 403.

> therefore,i found no logs about my first three commit-lint check. Is there any tips about recheck my lint error?

So I think you will have to deploy the common postgres chart also.
I don't tend to use helm install, rather the helm deploy plugin developed internally. This is also what is used in gating.

You can make all with:
make SKIP_LINT=TRUE all, then

use the following overrides to deploy the required components with the following cmd:

helm deploy onap local/onap --verbose --namespace onap --create-namespace --set global.masterPassword=test -f overrides_uui.yaml

########
overrides_uui.yaml

global:
  repository: nexus3.onap.org:10001
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  masterPassword: 'test'

  # Change to an unused port prefix range to prevent port conflicts
  # with other instances running within the same k8s cluster
  nodePortPrefix: 302
  nodePortPrefixExt: 304

  # ONAP Repository
  # Uncomment the following to enable the use of a single docker
  # repository but ONLY if your repository mirrors all ONAP
  # docker images. This includes all images from dockerhub and
  # any other repository that hosts images for ONAP components.
  #repository: nexus3.onap.org:10001
  repositoryCred:
    user: docker
    password: docker
  dockerHubRepository: docker.io

  busyboxRepository: docker.io

   # common global images
  # Busybox for simple shell manipulation
  busyboxImage: busybox:1.34.1

  # curl image
  curlImage: curlimages/curl:7.80.0

  # env substitution image
  envsubstImage: dibi/envsubst:1

  # generate htpasswd files image
  # there's only latest image for htpasswd
  htpasswdImage: xmartlabs/htpasswd:latest

  # kubenretes client image
  kubectlImage: bitnami/kubectl:1.22.4

  # logging agent
  loggingImage: beats/filebeat:5.5.0

  # mariadb client image
  mariadbImage: bitnami/mariadb:10.7.3

  # nginx server image
  nginxImage: bitnami/nginx:1.21.4

  # postgreSQL client and server image
  postgresImage: crunchydata/crunchy-postgres:centos8-13.2-4.6.1

  # readiness check image
  readinessImage: onap/oom/readiness:3.0.1

  # default mount path root directory referenced
  # by persistent volumes and log files
  persistence:
    mountPath: /dockerdata-nfs
    enableDefaultStorageclass: false
    parameters: {}
    storageclassProvisioner: kubernetes.io/no-provisioner
    volumeReclaimPolicy: Retain

  # override default resource limit flavor for all charts
  flavor: unlimited

  # flag to enable debugging - application support required
  debugEnabled: false

  # default password complexity
  # available options: phrase, name, pin, basic, short, medium, long, maximum security
  # More datails: https://masterpassword.app/masterpassword-algorithm.pdf
  passwordStrength: short

  # configuration to set log level to all components (the one that are using
  # "common.log.level" to set this)
  # can be overrided per components by setting logConfiguration.logLevelOverride
  # to the desired value
  logLevel: DEBUG

  clusterName: cluster.local

  #Global ingress configuration
  ingress:
    enabled: false
    virtualhost:
        enabled: true
        baseurl: "simpledemo.onap.org"

  # Global Service Mesh configuration
  # POC Mode, don't use it in production
  serviceMesh:
    enabled: false
    tls: true

  # Disabling AAF
  # POC Mode, only for use in development environment
  # Keep it enabled in production
  aafEnabled: true
  aafAgentImage: onap/aaf/aaf_agent:2.1.20

aaf:
  enabled: true
postgres:
  enabled: true
uui:
  enabled: true

i have tried my best to identify the lint problem , it logs:
```Release "onap-uui" does not exist. Installing it now.
Error: unable to build kubernetes objects from release manifest: error validating "": error validating data: ValidationError(Deployment.spec.template.spec.containers[0].volumeMounts[3]): missing required field "name" in io.k8s.api.core.v1.VolumeMount```

then i use
helm template onap local/onap --namespace onap  --set global.masterPassword=test -f /home/junyi/overrides_uui.yaml >/home/junyi/onap.yaml
to get my template. 
i figured it that my volumeMounts declared:
```         {{- if .Values.global.aafEnabled }}
          volumeMounts: {{ include "common.certInitializer.volumeMount" . | nindent 10 }}
          - mountPath: /uui/run.sh
            name: entrypoint
            subPath: run.sh
          - mountPath: /config/application.properties
            name: config
            subPath: application.properties
      volumes: {{ include "common.certInitializer.volumes" . | nindent 6 }}
      - name: config
        configMap:
          name: {{ include "common.fullname" . }}
      - name: entrypoint
        configMap:
          name: {{ include "common.fullname" . }}-entrypoint
          defaultMode: 0755
      {{- end }}
```
but the result shows that:
```          volumeMounts: 
          - mountPath: /opt/app/osaaf
            name: onap-uui-server-aaf-config
          - mountPath: /uui/run.sh
            name: entrypoint
            subPath: run.sh
          - mountPath: /config/application.properties
            name: config
            subPath: application.properties
          - mountPath: /
```

i don`t know why the last line came out without a mount name

```- mountPath: /```

i dont know how to solve this problem 

i tried to add:

```
          - mountPath: /
            name: empty
```

in my uui-server/template/deployment.yaml , but it didn`t work. the error occured as well.

if there is any solution or experience,please reply.

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/rtdv3-global-verify-master/13629/ : SUCCESS
